title: Caloncoba brevipes
taxonomy: Caloncoba brevipes belongs to the family Guttiferae, but it is now often classified under the family Ochnaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. Caloncoba brevipes is a genus of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The plant is primarily classified as a member of the order Ochanales, which includes other species of Ochnaceae family. Further classification details may vary depending on the source and the most up-to-date botanical research.
uses: Caloncoba brevipes is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing fever and relieving pain. In some parts of Africa, Caloncoba brevipes is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and bark are often used in medicinal preparations, which are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Caloncoba brevipes.

intro: Caloncoba brevipes, a medicinal plant species native to tropical Africa, is a member of the family Ochnaceae. The health benefits of Caloncoba brevipes include its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating various infections.
